message:
fix: ReadyChunks size_hint, Sleep custom clock polling, percent-decode escape

ReadyChunks/BufChunks size_hint (src/stream/chunks.rs):
- ReadyChunks lower bound now uses div_ceil instead of integer division,
  fixing off-by-one for partial chunks (e.g. 5 items / chunk_size 3
  now correctly reports lower=2 not lower=1)
- BufChunks accounts for buffered partial chunk in lower bound and
  allows per-item flushes in upper bound calculation
- Adds three new tests covering buffered chunks, guaranteed upstream
  items, and per-item flush scenarios

Sleep custom clock polling (src/time/sleep.rs):
- When a Sleep uses a custom time getter (logical/virtual clock), adds
  a 1ms poll interval fallback to check logical time frequently instead
  of sleeping until the wall-clock deadline
- Prevents futures from hanging indefinitely when injected logical
  clocks advance without wall-clock progression
- Adds test verifying prompt wakeup after logical time advance

Percent-decode fix (src/web/extract.rs):
- Invalid escape sequences (e.g. %1G) now increment index by 1 instead
  of 3, allowing the next character to be reprocessed as a potential
  new escape sequence start
- Fixes: %%41 now correctly decodes to %A (first % is incomplete
  escape, second % starts valid %41 → 'A')

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
